decisions.title: Opening Moves
decisions.text: War has come. The Rival's opening gambit is a rapid grab at the disputed approaches. How do you meet it?

options.label: Contest forward — deny the fait accompli at the water's edge.
Trade space for time; consolidate on prepared lines.
Open with deep strikes on their staging areas.

decisions.title: Mobilization
decisions.text: The conflict widens. Reserves, allies, and industry must be marshalled.

options.label: Prioritize operational tempo; go it alone if needed.

decisions.title: Escalation Management
decisions.text: The Rival signals it may widen the war unless you relent. Retaliatory reach is your leverage.

options.label: Hold escalation dominance; make continuation costly.
Offer an off-ramp to cap the war.
Press the advantage regardless of risk.

decisions.title: Termination
decisions.text: Both sides are exhausted. The terms of the settlement will be set now.

options.label: Leverage battlefield position for favorable terms.
Settle for restoration of the status quo ante.
Accept an unfavorable settlement to end the bleeding.

settlements.text: A favorable settlement: the Rival's aims are frustrated and the frontier restored on your terms — but at a cost that deterrence might have avoided.
A negotiated settlement roughly restoring the status quo ante, purchased in blood and treasure.
A costly, ambiguous outcome: the war ends with the frontier redrawn against you.
A strategic defeat: exhausted and outmaneuvered, you accept terms dictated by the Rival.
